name: CourtListener Monitor
failure_modes: 401 — token missing or expired. Regenerate at courtlistener.com/profile/
400 unknown_params — endpoint changed. Was /dockets/, now /search/?type=d
0 results — queries may need updating. Check SEARCH_TERMS list in courtlistener.py
Slow — page_size is 20, safely increase to 50 in PAGE_SIZE constant

name: FBI CDE Monitor
failure_modes: 403 MissingAuthenticationTokenException — key sent as header, must be URL param
404 Not Found — endpoint path wrong, check cde.ucr.cjis.gov for current API
Empty response — year parameter may be out of range, try current year - 1
0 records — OFFENSE_TYPES list in fbi_stats.py may use invalid slugs
notes: Aggregate data only. Key: vIGVtIYpjRh4oG6d8fI3SrAvdYUfCWXcsyCAyhXU. Must be ?api_key= param.

name: AP RSS Monitor
failure_modes: feeds.apnews.com — PERMANENTLY DEAD. Do not attempt to restore.
rsshub.app — RESTRICTED. Do not use.
NYT feed 403 — NYT may have paywalled their RSS
Guardian feed down — check theguardian.com/us-news/rss directly
0 results — check keyword filters in ap_rss.py are not too restrictive
notes: Now uses NYT/NPR/Guardian feeds. File still called ap_rss.py for historical reasons.

name: Congress RSS Monitor
failure_modes: Feed URLs changed — check https://www.congress.gov/rss/ for current paths
0 results — keyword filters may be too restrictive for legislative language
Feed parses but no violence content — Congress RSS covers legislation not prosecutions
notes: Returns legislative mentions of DV/trafficking. Low volume is normal.

name: ED.gov Monitor
failure_modes: 403 — endpoint moved or auth required. Check ocrcas.ed.gov for current API.
404 — URL structure changed. Check ed.gov developer docs.
Empty — issue parameter 14 may no longer map to sexual violence
notes: Currently returning 403. Needs investigation. Campus sexual violence cases.

name: DOJ Press Release Monitor
failure_modes: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — highest priority source to add
If implemented and 0: check feed URL, justice.gov occasionally restructures
notes: PRIORITY 1 unimplemented source. No auth required. Add sources/doj_press.py

name: Save Rate Pattern Monitor
description: Monitors ratio of saved/found cases each scan
diagnosis: Check source_url uniqueness — same URL across many records causes hash collisions
Check make_case_id() in record.py still includes source_url in hash
Check FBI source URLs — all records may share same CDE dashboard URL
If rate is 0: all records already in DB from previous scan — this is NORMAL

name: Type Distribution Monitor
description: Monitors violence type distribution for anomalies
diagnosis: One type dominating may indicate source bias (e.g. CourtListener heavy on DV dockets)
Check SEARCH_TERMS in courtlistener.py — may over-weight certain terms
Check Voss classification rules — a high-weight rule may be matching too broadly

name: Public Figure Rate Monitor
diagnosis: Rate too low: detect_public_figure() in record.py may be missing role keywords
Rate too high: public figure keywords may be too broad — 'officer' matches too many
Check Cairo's EXTENDED_PUBLIC_FIGURE_ROLES for false positives

name: Geographic Distribution Monitor
alert_condition: any US state with zero cases for 5+ consecutive scans
diagnosis: Underserved states list in Lira.py for context
State-specific sources (AG feeds) would help — see Reed's STATE_AG_FEEDS
Rural states genuinely have less documented cases — not always a data problem

name: Geocoding Health Monitor
alert_condition: cases with null lat/lng > 20% of total
diagnosis: Nominatim may be rate-limiting — check 0.2s sleep is present in scanner.py
DNS issue — test: nslookup nominatim.openstreetmap.org 8.8.8.8
Invalid city names slipping through — check _INVALID_CITIES in record.py
State fallback coordinates should catch most failures via STATE_COORDS in scanner.py

name: Status Distribution Monitor
diagnosis: Check _infer_status() in each source module
CourtListener: dateTerminated set → should be convicted or acquitted
News RSS: 'sentenced', 'convicted', 'acquitted' keywords → update status
FBI: all records should be 'reported' not 'unknown'

name: Verification Rate Monitor
diagnosis: Check Maren's TRUSTED_DOMAINS — new source domains may need adding
Aggregate sources (FBI) are always unverified — this is correct
If CourtListener records show unverified: check domain in Maren

name: Duplicate Detection Monitor
diagnosis: High duplicate rate: multiple sources reporting same cases — normal for high-profile
Very high rate (>40%): check if case_id hash is too aggressive
Check make_case_id() — source_url must be included for uniqueness

name: Database Connection Monitor
checks: Engine is singleton (_engine global in database.py)
failure_modes: KeyboardInterrupt on rollback — engine created per-call, not singleton. Fix: use _engine global.
SSL error — Neon requires SSL. Check connection string includes sslmode=require.
Connection timeout — check pool_recycle setting, check Neon dashboard for outages.

name: Environment Variable Monitor
notes: Env vars lost on Termux restart unless added to ~/.bashrc

name: DNS Health Monitor
notes: Termux DNS can break while IP connectivity works. Fix resolv.conf. Check Android Private DNS settings if fix doesn't hold.

name: Termux Process Monitor
notes: Android kills background processes. termux-wake-lock prevents this during scans.
